name: 周期性
subject: chemistry
domain_name: Unit 1-2 原子与化学键 Atoms & Bonding
curriculum_points:
TOPIC 1.2
Mass Spectra of Elements

LEARNING OBJECTIVE
1.2.A
Explain the quantitative relationship between the mass spectrum of an element and the masses of the element’s isotopes.

ESSENTIAL KNOWLEDGE
1.2.A.1
The mass spectrum of a sample containing a single element can be used to determine the identity of the isotopes of that element and the relative abundance of each isotope in nature.
1.2.A.2
The average atomic mass of an element can be estimated from the weighted average of the isotopic masses using the mass of each isotope and its relative abundance.

TOPIC 1.6
Photoelectron Spectroscopy

LEARNING OBJECTIVE
1.6.A
Explain the relationship between the photoelectron spectrum of an atom or ion and:
i.	The ground-state electron configuration of the species.
ii. The interactions between the electrons and the nucleus.

ESSENTIAL KNOWLEDGE
1.6.A.1
The energies of the electrons in a given shell can be measured experimentally with photoelectron spectroscopy (PES). The position of each peak in the PES spectrum is related to the energy required to remove an electron from the corresponding subshell, and the relative height of each peak is (ideally) proportional to the number of electrons in that subshell.

errors.description: 只记年代与名词，不能用史料与制度变迁解释历史进程。
把传说叙述与考古/文献证据混为一谈，未区分证据强度。

exercises.stem: 结合课标要求，说明「周期性」的核心内涵，并各举一条史料或史实依据。
